title: Climate-proofing runway capacity at a major hub
summary: An airport used C3S projections to quantify rising heat-restriction days and justify a runway-capacity study.
challenge: Rising temperatures reduce air density and aircraft take-off performance. The airport's runway-length assumptions were based on historical temperature distributions that no longer reflected the climate trajectory, risking unplanned weight restrictions on long-haul departures.
approach: C3S downscaled projections modelled summer temperature-exceedance frequencies to mid-century under multiple scenarios, validated against ERA5. Results fed aircraft-performance models for a range of long-haul types to quantify payload-penalty days per year.
results: Analysis showed a marked increase in high-temperature restriction days by the 2040s. The airport used the outputs to justify a runway-extension feasibility study and updated its master plan with climate-adjusted capacity assumptions.
